New Clawed Code update is insane. Anthropic just dropped a crazy update to Clawude code. And honestly, this one changes everything. You can now control your AI coding agent from your phone while it keeps building stuff on your computer without you even being there. And that's not even the wildest part. Today, I'm going to show you exactly how this new Clawude code update works. And we're talking remote control from your pocket, deep thinking mode that makes the AI way smarter, and a bunch of power tips that most people don't know about yet. This is brand new. It's a gamecher and it's super easy to use. Let's get into it. So, first, what even is Claude Code real quick? Claude Code is a tool made by Anthropic. You know, the company behind Claude, but instead of chatting with AI in a browser like most people do, Claude Code lives inside your terminal, your command line, the place where real developers do their work. And it doesn't just talk to you. It actually does stuff. It can read your files, write code, run commands, fix bugs, refactor entire projects, build full applications from scratch. Think of it like having an AI developer sitting inside your computer ready to work whenever you tell it to. It launched in 2025 and it blew up fast. Developers went crazy for it because it wasn't just another chatbot. It was an actual coding agent, one that could take action, not just give suggestions. Okay, so now let's talk about what just changed because this update has two massive new features. Number one, remote control. You can now control Claude code from your phone. Number two, effort max mode, a deep reasoning mode that makes Claude think way harder about tough problems. Let me break both of these down because once you see what they do, you're going to want to try this immediately. Let's start with remote control. This is the big one. Here's what it does. You start a clawed code session on your computer, your laptop, your desktop, whatever. Then you run a simple command. Claude remote control uh QR code pops up. You scan it with your phone and boom, you're now controlling your AI coding agent from your phone from anywhere. Your phone becomes the remote for your AI developer. You can start builds, monitor what it's doing, approve file changes, send new instructions, watch logs in real time, keep the conversation going, all from your pocket. And here's the part that matters most. Your code never leaves your machine. Only the messages go through anthropic servers. The AI keeps running locally on your computer. Your code stays safe. Your projects stay private. Think of it like a walkie-talkie to your coding agent. You're just sending it instructions. The heavy lifting still happens on your machine. Now, why is this such a huge deal? Before this update, if you were using any AI coding tool, you had to sit at your computer the whole time. If the AI was doing a big refactor or running a long build, you just had to sit there and wait, staring at your screen, doing nothing. Now you can literally walk away, start a big refactor, go grab lunch, check your phone, see how it's going, send a new instruction if you need to. Come back to your desk and everything's done. This is massive for anyone doing real development work, longunning builds, big code changes, production debugging. You don't have to be chained to your desk anymore. Your AI developer keeps working while you live your life. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Okay, so let me give you some real examples of how people are using remote control right now. Developers are using it to monitor CI builds from their phone. They're kicking off huge code refactors and then going for a walk. They're managing long running tasks without sitting at their desk. They're debugging production issues from their couch. Some people are literally starting coding sessions at work and then continuing them on the train home. One person on Reddit said it perfectly. Start a cla code session locally. Continue it from your phone. That's the dream right there. And now it's real. Now, let's talk about the second big feature. Effort max mode. This one is more technical, but it's super powerful. Claude code now has effort levels. You can tell it how hard to think about a problem. You type slasheffort and then pick a level, low, medium, or max. When you set it to max, you're basically telling Claude, "Hey, spend more time on this. Think deeper. Don't rush it." And it actually listens. It uses more tokens. It does deeper analysis. It thinks through problems step by step. It catches things it would normally miss. This is perfect for the hard stuff. race conditions. Those bugs where things work fine most of the time but randomly break. Architecture decisions where you need to think about the big picture. Complex debugging where the problem could be anywhere. Large refactors where one wrong move breaks everything. Algorithm problems that need careful logic. Let me give you an example. Without effort max, you might say fix this bug and claw will take a quick look and give you a fix. Maybe it works, maybe it doesn't. But with effort max you say / effort max investigate why the async q sometimes duplicates jobs. And now claude goes deep. It looks at concurrency, thread safety, race conditions, the entire execution flow. It thinks through every possibility and it gives you a real answer, not a guess. This is the difference between a quick fix and actually understanding the problem. And for serious developers, that matters a lot. Now, here's where it gets really good. I want to give you some power tips that most people don't know about yet. These will make Claude Code way more effective for you. And by the way, if you want to learn how to use AI tools like Claude Code to automate your workflows and scale your business, join the AI profit boardroom. It's the best place to learn how to use cuttingedge AI tools like Claude Code to streamline your work and get more done. Link is in the description. Okay, so power tip number one, use a claw.md file. This is a special config file you put in your project. It tells Claude everything about your codebase before it starts working. Your project structure, your coding rules, what tools you use, how your architecture works. Think of it like an onboarding dock for your AI developer. The better your Claud MD file, the better Claude performs. It's like giving a new hire a really good training manual on day one. Power tip number two, keep your repo clean and structured. Claude works way better when your code is organized. Clear folder names, good documentation, readable code. If your code base is a mess, Claude's going to struggle just like a human developer would. Clean code in, clean code out. Power tip number three, use agent workflows. This is where it gets wild. You can tell Claude to do an entire pipeline. Build the back end. Write the tests, run the tests, fix the failing tests, deploy, all in one go. It just works through the list step by step like a real developer would, except it doesn't get tired. It doesn't get distracted. It just keeps going. Now, let me be real with you about the limitations because nothing is perfect, and I'm not going to pretend it is. First, remote control currently needs GitHub authentication on your phone. That's for security and repo access. The team says this is temporary, but right now it's a requirement. Second, each clawed instance supports one remote session per process. So you can't have five people controlling the same session unless you use server mode. Third, if you close your terminal, the session ends. So keep that terminal window open. Don't accidentally close it, lose your progress. And fourth, right now, this is mainly available for Max users. Is still in a research preview stage, so it might not be available to everyone just yet. But even with those limitations, this update is huge. And here's why. We're watching AI coding tools evolve in real time. They started as simple code completion tools. Then they became chat assistants. Then they became agents that could actually write and run code. And now now they're becoming autonomous developers you can manage from your pocket. The old way was chat. Get some code, copy it, paste it, run it, hope it works. The new way is tell your AI agent what to do and it does the whole thing inside your machine autonomously while you go live your life. That's a massive shift. We're going from AI assistants to AI agents, from tools that help you code to tools that code for you. And Claude Code with this new update is right at the front of that shift. The AI coding space is getting wild right now. You've got Cursor, GitHub C-Pilot, OpenClaw, and now Claude Code pushing the boundaries even further with remote control and deep reasoning. The competition is fierce, and that's great for all of us because it means these tools keep getting better and better. So, here's my take. If you're a developer or you work with developers or you run any kind of tech business, you need to pay attention to claw code. This update is not just a small improvement. It's a signal of where everything is heading. AI developers you can manage from anywhere. That's the future and it's happening right now.
